channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Musk asks X if he should rehire DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk took to his social media platform X to ask users if he should rehire one of his deputies who resigned Thursday after racist social media posts of his resurfaced.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Can’t buy an Nvidia RTX 5080 or 5090 GPU due to stock shortages? If you were hoping GeForce Now might provide a good alternative, we’ve got some bad news
item.description: Nvidia GeForce Now goes from bad to worse for sign-ups, with no plans available at all, now – but day passes will return in a couple of weeks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Clínica Universidad de Navarra apuesta por la IA y la computación cuántica en el ámbito sanitario
item.description: La Clínica Universidad de Navarra (CUN) y Microsoft Ibérica han firmado un acuerdo de colaboración para promover el desarrollo conjunto de proyectos innovadores en el sector salud, aplicando para ello tecnologías avanzadas como la inteligencia artificial (IA) o la computación cuántica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spy vs spy: Security agencies help secure the network edge
item.description: The national intelligence services of five countries have offered enterprises advice on beating spies at their own game in a series of documents intended to help them protect network edge devices and appliances such as firewalls, routers, VPN (virtual private networks) gateways, internet of things (
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Smart Traffic Enforcement: Kazakhstan’s Qorgau System in Action
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Qorgau: How Kazakhstan is Using AI to Make Roads Safer
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS Redshift Security Improvements: A Welcome Addition
item.description: AWS Redshift security improvements welcome addition
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rust update fixes ‘forever’ compilation
item.description: The Rust Team has published a point release update, Rust 1.84.1, to address a few regressions in last month’s Rust 1.84 release, including fixing painfully slow compilation related to the next-generation trait solver.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Automated builds, tests, and quality gates are key to software quality – report
item.description: When it comes to software quality, the top three practices cited for production readiness include automated building and deploying, automated tests, and automated quality gates for every merge request, according to the JetBrains Qodana State of Software Quality Report 2024.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence
item.description: "I think we think of AI as primarily a bunch of the software geeks who are out there coding somewhere, but there's real hardware involved," said Neil Chilson. The post A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success
item.description: Louis Koplin, the acting program executive officer for the Navy’s PEO-Digital, said priorities for 2025 include cyber resiliency and expanding BYOD. The post Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Europe’s DMA gives another big boost to iOS platform decay
item.description: One of a handful of “independent” iOS app stores in Europe has begun the distribution of a porn app, advertising it as “Apple Approved.” The app isn’t approved by Apple, and the biggest app available on the AltStore is Epic’s child-focused Fortnite game.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops
item.description: "We use millimeter technology to collect images of passengers to ensure that no threats that are not allowed on the plane," said John Fortune The post DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Rising crypto popularity sparks surge in scams: Avoid falling prey
item.description: As cryptocurrency gains popularity, it also becomes a prime target for scammers, making it crucial to take precautions and avoid falling prey to scams.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Joyce, NewsNation

item.title: Not seeing ROI from your AI? Observability may be the missing link
item.description: From chatbots, to coding copilots, to AI agents, generative AI-powered apps are seeing increased traction among enterprises. As they go mainstream, however, their shortcomings are becoming more clear and problematic.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AMD patches microcode security holes after accidental early disclosure
item.description: AMD on Monday issued two patches for severe microcode security flaws, defects that AMD said “could lead to the loss of Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) protection.” The bugs were inadvertently revealed by a partner last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: I’m a tech expert: 10 AI prompts you’ll use all the time
item.description: From proofreading and research to brainstorming and data analysis, you can use generative AI as a sidekick for just about any task you can dream of.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big rigs deliver cargo with no humans at the wheel
item.description: First driverless semi delivers 100 loads in Texas, transforming logistics with AI. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
